ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for bánh ướt with ham. Closest parent is rice crepe food product (FOODON_03315763).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for bánh ướt with ham. A0CKP (rice-based flat bread/pancake) used as closest parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Vietnamese steamed rice roll dish.

allergens: gluten (from soy sauce in dipping sauce)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of bánh ướt with ham is approximately 200 g, comprising two to three steamed rice rolls topped with sliced Vietnamese-style pork ham, scallion oil, and served with a fish sauce dipping condiment.

nutrient_source.title: Bánh Ướt (Steamed Rice Roll) — component-based estimation using USDA FoodData Central rice flour (ID 169714) and Vietnamese pork cold cut analogue (ID 167903), cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Southeast Asian rice-based dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for bánh ướt with ham exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values estimated from primary components: steamed rice flour sheet (~65% by weight), Vietnamese-style pork ham/cold cut (~20% by weight), scallion oil (~5%), and fish sauce dipping (~10%). Rice flour nutritional data from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (ID 169714); pork luncheon meat/cold cut analogue from USDA FoodData Central (ID 167903). Weighted composite calculation applied. Values represent the assembled dish per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — steamed rice-based products and rice flour analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh ướt with ham. GI estimated at 68 based on analogous steamed rice flour-based products (plain rice noodles / rice vermicelli GI ~58–72, median ~65; steamed rice sheet GI ~68) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Am J Clin Nutr). The dominant carbohydrate source is gelatinised rice starch from the steamed rice flour sheet, which has a medium GI consistent with other steamed rice-based Southeast Asian preparations. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (68 × 43.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 29.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from Vietnamese pork ham and fish sauce dipping condiment
hypertension.guidance: A 200 g serving provides approximately 840 mg sodium (~37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), primarily from the processed pork ham and fish sauce; individuals with hypertension should request reduced dipping sauce and limit portion size to one roll.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (68) from gelatinised rice starch with moderate carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of 68 and a glycemic load of approximately 29 per standard serving, bánh ướt with ham produces a moderate postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one serving, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and monitor blood glucose accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Processed pork ham contributing saturated fat and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The processed pork ham component contributes saturated fat (~1.3 g/100 g) and high sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose leaner protein alternatives or reduce ham quantity, and minimise fish sauce consumption to lower overall sodium and saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh ướt with ham (bánh ướt thịt nguội) is a traditional Vietnamese dish consisting of thin, silky steamed rice flour sheets — similar to a delicate rice crepe — topped with sliced Vietnamese-style pork cold cut (thịt nguội), drizzled with fragrant scallion oil, and served alongside a light fish sauce-based dipping condiment (nước chấm). The rice sheets are made from a diluted rice flour batter steamed in thin layers until translucent and tender, yielding a soft, slightly chewy texture with a mild, neutral flavour that absorbs the savoury accompaniments. In Singapore, this dish is served at Vietnamese restaurants as a light meal or snack, reflecting the city-state's diverse Southeast Asian culinary landscape. Nutritionally, bánh ướt with ham is a moderate-energy dish (~148 kcal/100 g) with a carbohydrate-dominant macronutrient profile from gelatinised rice starch, moderate protein from the pork ham, and low fat. The sodium content is elevated due to the processed ham and fish sauce, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The medium glycemic index (~68) warrants mindful consumption for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.
common_user_queries: How many calories in bánh ướt with ham?
What is banh uot made of?
Is banh uot suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for bánh ướt with ham exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated from primary component data (USDA FoodData Central rice flour and pork cold cut entries) using weighted composition. GI was estimated from analogous steamed rice-based products in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level estimation is grounded in approved primary sources and the dish composition is well-characterised.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for rice flour (ID 169714) and pork cold cut analogue (ID 167903); no exact dish-level entry was found in any approved primary regional source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 4. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g; this is confirmed zero based on the natural composition of steamed rice flour and minimally processed pork ham with no hydrogenated fat ingredients. 5. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on steamed rice flour sheet products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement for bánh ướt with ham was located. 6. The dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork. 7. Allergen profile assumes standard preparation with fish sauce dipping condiment; allergen status may vary by restaurant. 8. The food_name has been corrected from the input 'Thin Pancake & Ham (Banh Uot)' to the canonical form 'Bánh Ướt with Ham (Steamed Rice Roll with Ham)' per Veritas naming rules. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from component-level USDA data and should be treated as approximate.
